2604.28093.title: What Makes a Good Terminal-Agent Benchmark Task: A Guideline for Adversarial, Difficult, and Legible Evaluation Design
2604.28093.paper_summary: Terminal-agent benchmarks have become a primary signal for measuring the coding and system-administration capabilities of large language models. As the market for evaluation environments grows, so does the pressure to ship tasks quickly, often without thorough adversarial review of the verification logic. This paper is a guideline for writing good benchmark tasks, drawn from over a year of contributing to and reviewing tasks for Terminal Bench. Most people write benchmark tasks the way they write prompts. They shouldn't. A prompt is designed to help the agent succeed; a benchmark is designed to find out if it can. We argue that good tasks are adversarial, difficult, and legible, and that a large class of common failure modes -- AI-generated instructions, over-prescriptive specifications, clerical difficulty, oracle solutions that assume hidden knowledge, tests that validate the wrong things, and reward-hackable environments -- are predictable consequences of treating task authoring as prompt authoring. We catalog these failure modes, argue that real difficulty is conceptual rather than environmental, and discuss recent empirical evidence that over 15% of tasks in popular terminal-agent benchmarks are reward-hackable. We hope this serves as a useful reference for benchmark maintainers, task contributors, and researchers using benchmark scores as evidence.
2604.28093.paper_summary_zh: 終端代理基準已成為衡量大型語言模型編碼和系統管理能力的主要指標。隨著評估環境市場的增長，快速交付任務的壓力也隨之增加，通常在沒有徹底對驗證邏輯進行對抗性審查的情況下進行。本文是撰寫良好基準任務的指導方針，基於一年多來對終端基準任務的貢獻和審查。大多數人撰寫基準任務的方式與撰寫提示的方式相同，但這樣不應該。提示旨在幫助代理成功；基準旨在找出它是否能夠成功。我們認為良好的任務應具備對抗性、困難性和可讀性，並且一大類常見的失敗模式——AI生成的指令、過於具體的規範、文書難度、假設隱藏知識的神諭解決方案、驗證錯誤事物的測試，以及可獎勵操控的環境——是將任務創作視為提示創作的可預測後果。我們對這些失敗模式進行了編目，並主張真正的困難是概念性的而非環境性的，並討論了最近的實證證據，顯示在流行的終端代理基準中超過15%的任務是可獎勵操控的。我們希望這能成為基準維護者、任務貢獻者和使用基準分數作為證據的研究人員的有用參考。
2604.28093.author: Ivan Bercovich et.al.
